title: Classifying with BERT
concept: To classify text with BERT, we append a special '[CLS]' token to the start; the model's output at this token position summarizes the whole sentence.

exercises.prompt: How does BERT know where to look to get a summary of a whole sentence? a:

options.text: A special [END] token at the end
A special [CLS] token at the beginning

exercises.explain: To classify text, BERT uses a special token named [CLS] (short for Classification) placed at the start of the text.

cards.title: Secrets of Text Sorting
cards.body: How does a super-smart AI model read a whole paragraph and instantly know if it's happy, sad, or spam? It doesn't guess; it uses a clever shortcut right at the start of the sentence! Let's learn how BERT summarizes everything into one single point.

cards.title: The [CLS] Token
cards.body: BERT is a powerful AI that reads sentences. To use it for classification (like sorting spam emails), we insert a special marker called **[CLS]** (classification) at the very start of our text. BERT processes the whole sentence and packs the overall summary into the output of this single marker.

cards.title: The Sticky Note Trick
cards.body: Imagine you are sending a long, detailed letter to a busy executive. Instead of making them read the whole letter, you attach a sticky note right at the very beginning (our [CLS] token) that summarizes the main point. The executive only needs to read that single sticky note to understand the mood and purpose of the entire message!

cards.title: BERT in Action
cards.body:
If you want BERT to analyze the sentiment of: 
`"I love this app!"`

You actually feed BERT:
`"[CLS] I love this app!"`

After reading it, BERT outputs a vector at the first position that represents the positive energy of the entire sentence!

cards.title: Do We Average the Words?
cards.body: Some people think BERT needs to average the meanings of all words in a sentence to classify it. In reality, BERT is trained to gather and focus the entire sentence's meaning directly into the single **[CLS]** token at the front!

cards.title: How BERT Sees the Input
cards.body: Notice how the [CLS] token acts as a bucket collecting the mood of the entire sentence, whereas normal tokens just process individual words.

left.text: Aggregates the entire sentence meaning for classification (e.g., "Positive Sentiment").

right.text: Focus on individual word meanings and grammar relationships in the sentence.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of the [CLS] token's output in BERT?

options.text: It masks random words so BERT can guess them.
It summarizes the entire sentence's meaning into one place.
It marks where one sentence ends and another begins.
It translates the sentence into another language.

exercises.explain: The [CLS] token's final output representation serves as the summary of the entire sentence for classification tasks.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The [CLS] token is placed at the start of a sentence because BERT uses it to gather a summary of the entire text.
exercises.explain: BERT uses the output representation of the [CLS] token (at the very beginning) to classify the whole sentence.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing special token that starts a sentence for BERT classification:
exercises.sentence: To classify text, BERT expects the _____ token at the beginning of the sequence.
exercises.explain: The [CLS] token must be appended at the start of the sentence so BERT can use its output position for text classification.

exercises.prompt: Which input format is correct for classifying a movie review with BERT?

options.text: Option B: "[CLS] This movie is amazing!"

exercises.explain: Only Option B shows the special [CLS] token correctly prepended at the beginning of the input sequence.

exercises.prompt: You want to train a BERT model to detect whether customer reviews are positive or negative. Where must you place the special '[CLS]' token in your input text?

options.text: At the very beginning (index 0) of the sentence
At the very end of the sentence, after the final word
Right in the middle to balance the sentence
In place of the main verb or action word

exercises.explain: To classify an entire text sequence, BERT expects the '[CLS]' token at the very beginning of the input (index 0). The final hidden state representation of this token is then passed to a classification layer.
